title: Python Setup & Basics
description: Install Python, set up virtual environment, and run your first script.
code:
# hello.py
print("Hello, Python!")
print(f"Python version: {sys.version}")
objectives: Write and execute your first Python script
exercises: Write a script that prints your name
Run a Python script from terminal

title: Data Types & Variables
description: Master Python's fundamental data types and learn how to work with variables effectively.
code:
# Dynamic typing
x = 42
name = "Alice"
scores = [95, 87, 91]
person = {"name": "Bob", "age": 30}

title: Control Flow
description: Control program execution with conditional statements and loops.
exercises: Create a loop that prints numbers

title: Functions
description: Create reusable code blocks with functions, parameters, and return values.
code:
def greet(name, greeting="Hello"):
    return f"{greeting}, {name}!"

result = greet("Alice", "Hi")
print(result)

title: File I/O Operations
description: Read from and write to files using Python's file handling capabilities.
code:
with open('data.txt', 'r') as file:
    content = file.read()

with open('output.txt', 'w') as file:
    file.write("Hello, World!")
exercises: Read content from a text file

title: Error Handling
description: Use try/except blocks to handle exceptions gracefully.

title: Modules & Packages
description: Import and use modules, create your own packages.
code:
import math
from datetime import datetime
import numpy as np

title: OOP Basics
description: Create classes, objects, and understand inheritance.

title: Advanced OOP
description: Learn about encapsulation, polymorphism, and magic methods.

title: Decorators
description: Use decorators to modify function behavior.

title: Generators & Iterators
description: Create memory-efficient generators using yield.

title: List Comprehensions
description: Advanced list comprehensions with conditions.
code:
matrix = [[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6], [7, 8, 9]]
flattened = [num for row in matrix for num in row]
primes = [x for x in range(2, 50) if all(x % y != 0 for y in range(2, int(x**0.5)+1))]

title: Lambda Functions
description: Create anonymous functions with lambda.
code:
add = lambda x, y: x + y
numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
squared = list(map(lambda x: x**2, numbers))
even = list(filter(lambda x: x % 2 == 0, numbers))

title: Working with JSON
description: Parse and generate JSON data.
code:
import json

data = {"name": "Alice", "age": 30}
json_str = json.dumps(data)
parsed = json.loads(json_str)

title: Regular Expressions
description: Pattern matching with Python's re module.
code:
import re

text = "Contact us at support@example.com or sales@company.org"
emails = re.findall(r'[\w\.-]+@[\w\.-]+\.\w+', text)

title: Unit Testing
description: Write tests with unittest module.

title: pytest Framework
description: Advanced testing with pytest.

title: Working with APIs
description: Make HTTP requests using requests library.
code:
import requests

response = requests.get('https://api.github.com')
data = {"key": "value"}
response = requests.post('https://httpbin.org/post', json=data)

title: Web Scraping
description: Extract data from websites with BeautifulSoup.
code:
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

url = 'https://example.com'
response = requests.get(url)
soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')
links = soup.find_all('a')

title: Database Operations
description: Connect to SQLite database and execute queries.
code:
import sqlite3

conn = sqlite3.connect('example.db')
cursor = conn.cursor()
cursor.execute('''CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT)''')

title: Async Programming
description: Write asynchronous code with async/await.
code:
import asyncio

async def fetch_data():
    print("Fetching data...")
    await asyncio.sleep(2)
    print("Data fetched!")
    return {"data": "sample"}

title: Flask Web Framework
description: Build a simple web application with Flask.

title: FastAPI Framework
description: Create high-performance APIs with FastAPI.

title: Data Analysis with Pandas
description: Manipulate and analyze data using Pandas.
code:
import pandas as pd

data = {'Name': ['Alice', 'Bob', 'Charlie'], 'Age': [25, 30, 35]}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
print(df.head())

title: Data Visualization
description: Create charts and graphs with Matplotlib.
code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

x = np.linspace(0, 10, 100)
y = np.sin(x)

plt.plot(x, y)
plt.show()

title: NumPy Arrays
description: Work with numerical arrays using NumPy.
code:
import numpy as np

arr1 = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
arr2 = np.zeros((3, 3))
arr3 = np.random.rand(5, 5)

title: Machine Learning Basics
description: Introduction to scikit-learn for ML.
code:
from sklearn import datasets
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression

diabetes = datasets.load_diabetes()
X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(diabetes.data, diabetes.target)
model = LinearRegression()
model.fit(X_train, y_train)

title: Docker for Python
description: Containerize Python applications with Docker.
code:
# Dockerfile
FROM python:3.9-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
COPY . .
CMD ["python", "app.py"]

title: CI/CD Pipeline
description: Set up continuous integration with GitHub Actions.
code:
# .github/workflows/python.yml
name: Python CI

on: [push]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v2
    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v2

title: Final Project
description: Build a complete Python application from scratch.
code:
# Project structure
myapp/
├── app/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── main.py
│   └── utils.py
├── tests/
├── requirements.txt
├── Dockerfile
└── README.md
